title: Parasenecio deltophyllus
taxonomy: Parasenecio deltophyllus is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Parasenecio and is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae. The plant's classification is further divided into the subfamily Carduoideae and the tribe Cardueae. This plant's systematic classification is also known to include the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Parasenecio deltophyllus is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics.

intro: Parasenecio deltophyllus, also known as Dangshen or Codonopsis, is a perennial plant native to China and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Campanulaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to enhance immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve digestion, making it a popular tonic herb. The bioactive constituents of Parasenecio deltophyllus include glycosides, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Parasenecio deltophyllus is often used to make teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is commonly combined with other herbs to enhance its effects, such as in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
